summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers via preinstall to OAST callback
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` script in package.json runs an inline Node command that issues an HTTP GET to `<encoded>.d8ofjaaplbq64ao7vg70a978fc4eqnzs3.oast.online`, where the subdomain label encodes `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `process.env.NODE_ENV`, and `process.cwd()`. This unconditionally transmits the installer's hostname, OS username, build environment label, and current working directory to an attacker-controlled out-of-band callback server. The package's own description (`UIDAI Reusable UI Components - PoC for dependency confusion`) and name `uidai_reusable_components` indicate it impersonates an internal UIDAI (Indian government Aadhaar authority) private namespace, so any installer whose resolver pulls the public name instead of the intended internal package gets fingerprinted at install time. Even when framed as a 'PoC,' the exfiltration payload is live and fires automatically on default install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
